{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]特別展時に撮影。}} 大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|円筒埴輪・須恵器
{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては後円部において両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、墳丘主軸と直交して南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|岩橋千塚|1967}}。 * 石室全長：7.8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ3.15メートル（石棚高さ1.7-2.05メートル） * 玄室前道（通廊）：長さ0.5メートル、幅0.9メートル * 羨道：長さ3.4メートル、幅1.2メートル 羨道部は玄室の東寄りに接続し、西側の袖が広い片袖傾向の平面形である。石室の石材は結晶片岩。玄室には石棚（1箇所2枚）・石梁1本を伴い、岩橋千塚古墳群で特徴的な岩橋型横穴式石室になるが、岩橋型全体としては成立期で天井高は発達段階である。玄室床面には玉石を敷き詰めたと見られる。 == 関連施設 == * [[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]（和歌山市岩橋） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管。 * [[和歌山市立博物館]]（和歌山市湊本町） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=岩橋千塚|publisher=和歌山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岩橋千塚|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=大谷山古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|大谷山古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳 -特別史跡岩橋千塚古墳群追加指定に伴う発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丹野拓・米田文孝|year=2018|chapter=|title=紀国造家の実像をさぐる 岩橋千塚古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」126）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787718365|ref=}} == 外部リンク== * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ 大谷山22号墳（遺跡）]、[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ 大谷山22号墳（文化財）] - 和歌山市教育委員会「和歌山市の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:おおたにやまにしゆうにこうふん}} [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with "2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary<ref name="National Designation">{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}</ref><ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).</ref>. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approxim..."
revision.text:
2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]]

The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - 2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - {{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. 2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - 2016 (Heisei 28): Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - '''Otaniyama Tumulus No. 22''' (大谷山22号墳, Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Iwade and Narukami, Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (zenpōkōen-fun) and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, which is designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site (Japan)|ja|特別史跡}}.
== Overview == The tumulus is situated in the northwestern mountain range of the Iwasaka Senzuka Kofun Cluster, which was built in the Iwasaka mountain region of the Wakayama Plain, near the mouth of the Kinokawa River. Several excavations have been conducted on the site.
The mound has a keyhole shape oriented towards the west-northwest. It is built in two tiers on an oval-shaped base and measures approximately 68 meters in length, making it one of the larger kofun in Wakayama Prefecture. Unlike other tumuli, no fukiishi (cover stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, but an array of cylindrical haniwa, including morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and various representational haniwa (shield-shaped, lid-shaped, human-shaped, and animal-shaped) have been discovered. Notably, the cylindrical haniwa display characteristics from both Group IV (Kii-type) and Group V (Kinai-type), which is a unique feature of this tumulus. Additionally, there is a separate, wide annex area at the constricted section of the mound.
The burial chamber is a side-entry stone chamber (yokoana-shiki sekishitsu) with a "Iwasaka-type" construction, featuring a stone shelf and stone beams. The total length of the stone chamber is 7.8 meters. Other artifacts discovered include Sue ware (MT15-TK10 type), haji ware, and Yayoi pottery. Based on these findings, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the late Kofun period.
This tumulus was designated as a Special Historic Site by the Japanese government in 1988, along with the Iwasaka Senzuka Kofun Cluster.
== Excavation History == * 1966 (Shōwa 41): Survey, interior chamber investigation, and excavation of the mound (Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University; report published in 1967). * 1988 (Shōwa 63): Official designation as a Special Historic Site as part of the Iwasaka Senzuka Kofun Cluster. * 2014 (Heisei 26): Measurement survey and mound excavation (Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education; report published in 2016). * 2016 (Heisei 28), October 3: Expansion of the designated historic site area.
== Mound Structure == The mound has the following dimensions: * Total mound length: Approximately 68 meters * Base platform - Oval-shaped * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * Rear circular section * Diameter: First tier 34.5-36.5 meters (slightly elliptical), second tier 24.5-27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: First tier approximately 2.9 meters, second tier approximately 5.1 meters * Front section * Length: First tier approximately 32.0 meters, second tier approximately 30.0 meters
There is speculation that some sections of the mound were originally exposed rock surfaces.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is located in the rear circular section and features a side-entry chamber with double side walls. It is oriented perpendicular to the mound’s main axis and opens towards the south. The chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber (Gen-shitsu): 4.1 meters in length, 2.5 meters in width, 3.15 meters in height (stone shelf height 1.7-2.05 meters) * Entrance corridor: 0.5 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width * Passageway (En-dō): 3.4 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width
The passageway is connected to the eastern side of the main chamber, with an asymmetrical side entrance, where the western side is broader. The chamber walls are made of crystalline schist. The Iwasaka-type stone chamber includes a stone shelf (comprising two slabs) and a single stone beam, typical of the Iwasaka Senzuka Kofun Cluster. The floor of the main chamber was likely paved with pebbles.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Kii Fudoki Hill|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Repository of excavated artifacts from Otaniyama Tumulus No. 22. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits and stores artifacts from Otaniyama Tumulus No. 22.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture Cultural Property Database|ja|和歌山県文化財データベース}}

Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part) | alternative_name = | affiliation = {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area) | location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 13 | latitude_sec = 38.37 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 13 | longitude_sec = 11.20 | iso = JP-30 | shape = {{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}} | size = Mound length 68m | burial_facility = Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams) | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery | associated_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | interred_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group" | tangible_cultural_property = | special_notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | icon = Zenpokoen-fun NW | label = Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}} and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi (cover stones)|ja|葺石}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Additional designation for the site's protection. == Mound Structure == * Mound length: approx. 68 meters * Base platform: oval shape * Length: approx. 80 meters * Height: approx. 4 meters * Rear Round Section: * Diameter: 34.5-36.5 meters (first tier), 24.5-27.5 meters (second tier) * Height: 2.9 meters (first tier), 5.1 meters (second tier) * Front Section: * Length: 32.0 meters (first tier), 30.0 meters (second tier) == Burial Chamber == The rear round section contains a double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: 4.1 meters (length), 2.5 meters (width), 3.15 meters (height) (stone shelf height: 1.7-2.05 meters) * Entrance passage: 3.4 meters (length), 1.2 meters (width) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Kii Fudoki no Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} - Houses excavated artifacts from Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Exhibits artifacts from Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun (Wakayama City Cultural Heritage)] [[Category:Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City]] [[Category:Zenpokoen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternative_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=13|latitude_sec=38.37|longitude_deg=135|longitude_min=13|longitude_sec=11.20|iso=JP-30|shape={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial_facility=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_cultural_property=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|icon=Zenpokoen-fun NW|label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|label_position=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternative_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=13|latitude_sec=38.37|longitude_deg=135|longitude_min=13|longitude_sec=11.20|iso=JP-30|shape={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial_facility=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_cultural_property=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|icon=Zenpokoen-fun NW|label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|label_position=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] - The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternative_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=13|latitude_sec=38.37|longitude_deg=135|longitude_min=13|longitude_sec=11.20|iso=JP-30|shape={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial_facility=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_cultural_property=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|icon=Zenpokoen-fun NW|label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|label_position=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] - The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: * Length: Approximately 80 meters * Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: * Diameter: * First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) * Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) * Height: * First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: * Length: * First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters * Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: * Length: 4.1 meters * Width: 2.5 meters * Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: * Length: 0.5 meters * Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: * Length: 3.4 meters * Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternative_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=13|latitude_sec=38.37|longitude_deg=135|longitude_min=13|longitude_sec=11.20|iso=JP-30|shape={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial_facility=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_cultural_property=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|icon=Zenpokoen-fun NW|label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|label_position=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue ware
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ginality]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]]

Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternative_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=13|latitude_sec=38.37|longitude_deg=135|longitude_min=13|longitude_sec=11.20|iso=JP-30|shape={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial_facility=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_cultural_property=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|icon=Zenpokoen-fun NW|label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|label_position=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially designated a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the designated historical site boundary{{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of Designation of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Heritage Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Cultural Property)] -
