Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special other_designation|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_designation|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially other_designation1 a Special other_designation in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special other_designation other_designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the other_designation1 historical site boundary{{other_designation Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of other_designations] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special other_designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{other_designation Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special other_designation|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_designation|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially other_designation1 a Special other_designation in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special other_designation other_designation for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the other_designation1 historical site boundary{{other_designation Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of other_designations] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special other_designation Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{other_designation Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" [[Category:kk]][[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}}

Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - (Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] {{citation needed|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}} {{Kofun navbar}}
