revision.comment: remarking some of the new pages
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section

{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" {{draft categories| {{citation needed|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] }}

The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" {{draft categories| {{citation needed|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] }}

Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" {{draft categories| {{citation needed|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama, Wakayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]][[Category:qq]] }}

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=draft|12=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation={{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otaniyama area)|location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates=34|coordinates=13|coordinates=38.37|coordinates=135|coordinates=13|coordinates=11.20|iso=JP-30|type={{ill|Zenpokoen-fun (Keyhole-shaped tomb)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Ancient tumulus|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the {{ill|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=Fukiishi (cover stones)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|ja|弥生土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * **Total Mound Length**: Approximately 68 meters * **Foundation (shield-shaped)**: ** Length: Approximately 80 meters ** Height: Approximately 4 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: ** Diameter: ** First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ** Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ** Height: ** First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * **Front Section**: ** Length: ** First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ** Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section

The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * **Total Chamber Length**: 7.8 meters * **Main Chamber (Genkan)**: ** Length: 4.1 meters ** Width: 2.5 meters ** Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * **Entrance Passage (Corridor)**: ** Length: 0.5 meters ** Width: 0.9 meters * **Antechamber (Seki-dō)**: ** Length: 3.4 meters ** Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor={{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大谷山22号墳 |画像 = [[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後円部、左に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（大谷山地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]岩橋・鳴神 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 38.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 13|経度秒 = 11.20 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長68m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西北西 |ラベル = 大谷山
22号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''大谷山22号墳'''（おおたにやまにじゅうにごうふん）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]岩橋・鳴神にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（国の[[特別史跡]]、うち大谷山地区）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]の大首長墓系譜 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |花山8号墳||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長52m||粘土床||4c末 |- |花山10号墳||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長44m||粘土槨
竪穴式石室||5c前半 |- |花山6号墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長60m||横穴式石室||5c末 |- |'''大谷山22号墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長68m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c前半 |- |[[大日山35号墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長86m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c前半 |- |[[天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|天王塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長88m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c中葉 |- |寺内57号墳||[[円墳]]||直径35-40m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c後半 |- |[[井辺1号墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺35m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c末-7c初頭 |}
和歌山県北西部、[[紀の川]]河口部の[[和歌山平野]]の岩橋山塊に営造された[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]のうち、北西の山塊に築造された古墳である。これまでに数次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西北西方向に向ける{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。墳丘は盾形の基壇上に2段築成で築造される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。墳丘長は約68メートルを測り{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}、和歌山県内では有数の規模になる和歌山県における主な古墳は次の通り（70メートルとする場合：{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/bcms/prefg/500700/haihu.pdf 天王塚山古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料]}} 和歌山県庁生涯学習局文化遺産課、2015年）。 : 1. [[天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|天王塚古墳]]（和歌山市吉礼・下和佐） - 墳丘長88メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群和佐地区。 : 2. [[車駕之古址古墳]]（和歌山市木ノ本） - 墳丘長86メートル。 : 2. [[大日山35号墳]]（和歌山市岩橋・井辺） - 墳丘長86メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群大日山地区。 : 4. [[井辺八幡山古墳]]（和歌山市井辺・森小手穂） - 墳丘長70メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群井辺前山地区。 : 4. '''大谷山22号墳'''（和歌山市岩橋・鳴神） - 墳丘長70メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群大谷山地区。 : 4. [[大谷古墳]]（和歌山市大谷） - 墳丘長70メートル。 。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められない一方、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・蓋形・人物形・動物形埴輪）が認められる{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。特に円筒埴輪はIV群系（紀伊型）・V群系（畿内型）が併存する点が注目される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。また墳丘くびれ部には[[造出]]状の広い別区がある{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。埋葬施設は後円部における両袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、全長7.8メートルを測る{{Sfn|大谷山古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。岩橋千塚古墳群に特徴的な岩橋型横穴式石室で、玄室に石棚・石梁を伴う。そのほか、埴輪以外の出土品として[[須恵器]]（MT15-TK10型式）・[[土師器]]・[[弥生土器]]などがある{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）に国の[[特別史跡]]に指定されている（特別史跡「岩橋千塚古墳群」に追加指定）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、測量調査・石室内部調査・墳丘発掘調査（和歌山市教育委員会・[[関西大学]]、[[1967年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|岩橋千塚|1967}}。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、国の[[特別史跡]]に指定（特別史跡「岩橋千塚古墳群」に追加指定）。 * [[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）、測量調査・墳丘発掘調査（和歌山県教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）10月3日、史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160622173820/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html 史跡等の指定等について]（文化庁報道発表、2016年6月17日）。。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約68メートル * 基壇 - 盾形。 ** 長さ：約80メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：1段目34.5-36.5メートル（やや楕円形）、2段目24.5-27.5メートル（楕円形） ** 高さ：1段目約2.9メートル、2段目約5.1メートル * 前方部： ** 長さ：1段目約32.0メートル、2段目約30.0メートル 一部では墳丘構築時から岩盤が露出していた可能性が推定される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂
{{small|中央に陥没坑。}} Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|家形埴輪
{{small|[[和歌山市立博物館]]展示。}} 大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|盾持人埴輪
{{small|和歌山市立博物館展示。}} 大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|[[双脚輪状文]]埴輪
{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]特別展時に撮影。}} 大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|円筒埴輪・須恵器
{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては後円部において両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、墳丘主軸と直交して南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|岩橋千塚|1967}}。 * 石室全長：7.8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ3.15メートル（石棚高さ1.7-2.05メートル） * 玄室前道（通廊）：長さ0.5メートル、幅0.9メートル * 羨道：長さ3.4メートル、幅1.2メートル 羨道部は玄室の東寄りに接続し、西側の袖が広い片袖傾向の平面形である。石室の石材は結晶片岩。玄室には石棚（1箇所2枚）・石梁1本を伴い、岩橋千塚古墳群で特徴的な岩橋型横穴式石室になるが、岩橋型全体としては成立期で天井高は発達段階である。玄室床面には玉石を敷き詰めたと見られる。 == 関連施設 == * [[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]（和歌山市岩橋） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管。 * [[和歌山市立博物館]]（和歌山市湊本町） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=岩橋千塚|publisher=和歌山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岩橋千塚|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=大谷山古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|大谷山古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳 -特別史跡岩橋千塚古墳群追加指定に伴う発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丹野拓・米田文孝|year=2018|chapter=|title=紀国造家の実像をさぐる 岩橋千塚古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」126）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787718365|ref=}} == 外部リンク== * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ 大谷山22号墳（遺跡）]、[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ 大谷山22号墳（文化財）] - 和歌山市教育委員会「和歌山市の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:おおたにやまにしゆうにこうふん}} [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]]

墳丘（右に後円部、左に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（大谷山地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]岩橋・鳴神 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 38.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 13|経度秒 = 11.20 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長68m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西北西 |ラベル = 大谷山
22号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''大谷山22号墳'''（おおたにやまにじゅうにごうふん）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]岩橋・鳴神にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（国の[[特別史跡]]、うち大谷山地区）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]の大首長墓系譜 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |花山8号墳||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長52m||粘土床||4c末 |- |花山10号墳||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長44m||粘土槨
竪穴式石室||5c前半 |- |花山6号墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長60m||横穴式石室||5c末 |- |'''大谷山22号墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長68m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c前半 |- |[[大日山35号墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長86m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c前半 |- |[[天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|天王塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長88m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c中葉 |- |寺内57号墳||[[円墳]]||直径35-40m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c後半 |- |[[井辺1号墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺35m||岩橋型横穴式石室||6c末-7c初頭 |}
和歌山県北西部、[[紀の川]]河口部の[[和歌山平野]]の岩橋山塊に営造された[[岩橋千塚古墳群]]のうち、北西の山塊に築造された古墳である。これまでに数次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西北西方向に向ける{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。墳丘は盾形の基壇上に2段築成で築造される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。墳丘長は約68メートルを測り{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}、和歌山県内では有数の規模になる和歌山県における主な古墳は次の通り（70メートルとする場合：{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/bcms/prefg/500700/haihu.pdf 天王塚山古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料]}} 和歌山県庁生涯学習局文化遺産課、2015年）。 : 1. [[天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|天王塚古墳]]（和歌山市吉礼・下和佐） - 墳丘長88メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群和佐地区。 : 2. [[車駕之古址古墳]]（和歌山市木ノ本） - 墳丘長86メートル。 : 2. [[大日山35号墳]]（和歌山市岩橋・井辺） - 墳丘長86メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群大日山地区。 : 4. [[井辺八幡山古墳]]（和歌山市井辺・森小手穂） - 墳丘長70メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群井辺前山地区。 : 4. '''大谷山22号墳'''（和歌山市岩橋・鳴神） - 墳丘長70メートル。岩橋千塚古墳群大谷山地区。 : 4. [[大谷古墳]]（和歌山市大谷） - 墳丘長70メートル。 。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められない一方、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・蓋形・人物形・動物形埴輪）が認められる{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。特に円筒埴輪はIV群系（紀伊型）・V群系（畿内型）が併存する点が注目される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。また墳丘くびれ部には[[造出]]状の広い別区がある{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。埋葬施設は後円部における両袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、全長7.8メートルを測る{{Sfn|大谷山古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。岩橋千塚古墳群に特徴的な岩橋型横穴式石室で、玄室に石棚・石梁を伴う。そのほか、埴輪以外の出土品として[[須恵器]]（MT15-TK10型式）・[[土師器]]・[[弥生土器]]などがある{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）に国の[[特別史跡]]に指定されている（特別史跡「岩橋千塚古墳群」に追加指定）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、測量調査・石室内部調査・墳丘発掘調査（和歌山市教育委員会・[[関西大学]]、[[1967年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|岩橋千塚|1967}}。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、国の[[特別史跡]]に指定（特別史跡「岩橋千塚古墳群」に追加指定）。 * [[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）、測量調査・墳丘発掘調査（和歌山県教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）10月3日、史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160622173820/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html 史跡等の指定等について]（文化庁報道発表、2016年6月17日）。。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約68メートル * 基壇 - 盾形。 ** 長さ：約80メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：1段目34.5-36.5メートル（やや楕円形）、2段目24.5-27.5メートル（楕円形） ** 高さ：1段目約2.9メートル、2段目約5.1メートル * 前方部： ** 長さ：1段目約32.0メートル、2段目約30.0メートル 一部では墳丘構築時から岩盤が露出していた可能性が推定される{{Sfn|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}。 Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂

{{small|和歌山市立博物館展示。}} 大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|[[双脚輪状文]]埴輪
{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]特別展時に撮影。}} 大谷山22号墳 出土品.JPG|円筒埴輪・須恵器
{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては後円部において両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、墳丘主軸と直交して南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|岩橋千塚|1967}}。 * 石室全長：7.8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ3.15メートル（石棚高さ1.7-2.05メートル） * 玄室前道（通廊）：長さ0.5メートル、幅0.9メートル * 羨道：長さ3.4メートル、幅1.2メートル 羨道部は玄室の東寄りに接続し、西側の袖が広い片袖傾向の平面形である。石室の石材は結晶片岩。玄室には石棚（1箇所2枚）・石梁1本を伴い、岩橋千塚古墳群で特徴的な岩橋型横穴式石室になるが、岩橋型全体としては成立期で天井高は発達段階である。玄室床面には玉石を敷き詰めたと見られる。 == 関連施設 == * [[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]（和歌山市岩橋） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管。 * [[和歌山市立博物館]]（和歌山市湊本町） - 大谷山22号墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=岩橋千塚|publisher=和歌山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岩橋千塚|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=大谷山古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|大谷山古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳 -特別史跡岩橋千塚古墳群追加指定に伴う発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丹野拓・米田文孝|year=2018|chapter=|title=紀国造家の実像をさぐる 岩橋千塚古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」126）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787718365|ref=}} == 外部リンク== * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ 大谷山22号墳（遺跡）]、[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ 大谷山22号墳（文化財）] - 和歌山市教育委員会「和歌山市の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:おおたにやまにしゆうにこうふん}} [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]]
