{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation=[[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] (Otaniyama area)|location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates={{Coord|34.227325|N|135.219778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso=JP-30|type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|qq=|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation=[[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] (Otaniyama area)|location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates={{Coord|34.227325|N|135.219778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso=JP-30|type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|qq=|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Wakayama City Board of Education "Wakayama City's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大谷山22号墳|version=104551574|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22]] [[ja:大谷山22号墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:大谷山22號墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q55452706}}

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation=[[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] (Otaniyama area)|location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates={{Coord|34.227325|N|135.219778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso=JP-30|type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|qq=|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|name=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|image=[[File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation=[[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] (Otaniyama area)|location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates={{Coord|34.227325|N|135.219778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso=JP-30|type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|qq=|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - Mound (right: rear round part, left: front part)|alternate_name=|affiliation=[[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]] (Otaniyama area)|location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], Iwasaka, Narukami|coordinates={{Coord|34.227325|N|135.219778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso=JP-30|type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|size=Mound length 68m|burial=Double-sided {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|qq=|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - (Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular section
{{small|Central sinkhole visible.}} File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Otaniyama Kofun Group-22, funkyu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:大谷山22号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 盾持人埴輪.JPG|Shield-bearing haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Wakayama City Museum.}} File:大谷山22号墳出土 双脚輪状文埴輪.JPG|Double-legged ring-pattern haniwa
{{small|Photographed at a special exhibition at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} File:大谷山22号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and Sue pottery
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History.}} == burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular section and opens southward, perpendicular to the mound's main axis. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun - Excavation Report for the Special Historic Site Expansion|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|author=Taku Tanno and Fumitaka Yoneda|year=2018|title=Exploring the Realities of the Kii Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Family: The Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group (Archaeological Series 126)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787718365}} == External Links == * {{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/7061/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (Archaeological Site)], [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/5151/ Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22 (designation1)] - (Iwasaka style, with stone shelves and stone beams)|burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery|associated_tombs=|built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]|interred_person=|imperial_tomb=|historic_site=Included in the {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group"|tangible_notes=|special_notes=|map=Japan Wakayama|type=Zenpokoen-fun NW|map_dot_label=Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun|map_dot_label=bottom|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Otaniyama No. 22 Kofun''' (大谷山22号墳, ''Ōtaniyama Nijūni-gōfun'') is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} by the Japanese government. == Overview == This kofun is located in the northwestern hills of the Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group, situated in the {{ill|Wakayama Plain|ja|和歌山平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Plain|1=Wakayama Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417361}} near the mouth of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]]. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tomb is a keyhole-shaped mound oriented west-northwest. The mound is built in a two-tiered construction on an oval-shaped base. The total mound length is approximately 68 meters, making it one of the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound exterior lacks [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi (cover stones)]] but features [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (including cylindrical and decorative haniwa) and various shaped haniwa (shield, lid, human, and animal figures). Notably, it includes both Type IV (Kii-style) and Type V (Kinai-style) cylindrical haniwa. The mound also features a separate leveled area at its constriction. The burial chamber is a double-sided corridor-style stone chamber located in the rear round section. It is approximately 7.8 meters in total length and exhibits the distinctive Iwasaka-style structure, featuring stone shelves and beams. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. This site was officially Historic Site1 a Special Historic Site in 1988 as part of the "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." == Excavation History == * '''1966''': Survey, internal stone chamber investigation, and mound excavation by Wakayama City Board of Education and Kansai University. * '''1988''': Included in the Special Historic Site Historic Site for "Iwasaka Chidzuka Kofun Group." * '''2014''': Survey and excavation conducted by Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education. * '''2016''': Expansion of the Historic Site1 historical site boundary{{Historic Site Online Database|401|2070|Iwahashi Chizuka Kofun Group}}[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2016061701.html Announcement of designation1 of Historic Sites] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, June 17, 2016).. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * '''Total Mound Length''': Approximately 68 meters * '''Foundation (shield-shaped)''': ''' Length: Approximately 80 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': ''' Diameter: ''' First Tier: 34.5–36.5 meters (slightly elliptical) ''' Second Tier: 24.5–27.5 meters (elliptical) ''' Height: ''' First Tier: Approximately 2.9 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 5.1 meters * '''Front Section''': ''' Length: ''' First Tier: Approximately 32.0 meters ''' Second Tier: Approximately 30.0 meters It has been suggested that parts of the mound's surface were exposed bedrock during its construction{{Sfn|Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun|2016}}. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}: * '''Total Chamber Length''': 7.8 meters * '''Main Chamber (Genkan)''': ''' Length: 4.1 meters ''' Width: 2.5 meters ''' Height: 3.15 meters (stone shelf height: 1.7–2.05 meters) * '''Entrance Passage (Corridor)''': ''' Length: 0.5 meters ''' Width: 0.9 meters * '''Antechamber (Seki-dō)''': ''' Length: 3.4 meters ''' Width: 1.2 meters The antechamber connects to the main chamber at an offset toward the east, with the west side being broader, displaying a single-sleeve tendency. The chamber walls are constructed of crystalline schist, and the main chamber includes one stone shelf (with two slabs in one location) and one stone beam. The design represents an early phase of the Iwahashi-style stone chamber, characterized by its high ceiling and developmental features. The chamber floor appears to have been paved with cobblestones. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Wakayama Prefectural Museum of Regional History]] (Iwahashi, Wakayama City) - Houses burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. * Wakayama City Museum (Minatohonmachi, Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Department of Archaeology|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=Iwahashi Chizuka|publisher=Wakayama City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iwahashi Chizuka|1967}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ōtaniyama Kofun Group|title=Japanese Historical Place names Series 31: Wakayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Ōtaniyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22, Tennōzuka Kofun -
